findingsNew.title: README still labels the Spec Kit section as Documentation instead of Framework
findingsNew.claim: README.md still uses the old Spec Kit Documentation label in both the TOC and heading, despite the active phase requiring Spec Kit Framework.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the README top feature table, which already says Spec Kit Framework at README.md:5, so the old label is localized drift rather than a global naming convention.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The old label could be intentionally narrower, but the active phase spec explicitly names the replacement.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: If the phase spec is amended to make the rename optional or preserve the old anchor intentionally, downgrade to P2.

findingsNew.title: Goal plugin is only documented as a Utility command instead of a full FEATURES subsection
findingsNew.claim: README.md does not give the mk-goal plugin equivalent FEATURES-section treatment, even though this phase requires a full Goal Plugin FEATURES subsection.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the plugin README for an existing detailed mk-goal contract and root README FEATURES headings for comparable core-system treatment.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Goal could be intentionally command-only in a general README, but the active phase scope explicitly says it should be promoted.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: If the phase spec is amended to keep Goal as command-only documentation, downgrade to P2.

findingDetails.title: README still labels the Spec Kit section as Documentation instead of Framework
findingDetails.scopeProof: README.md:33 and README.md:208 contain the old label; spec.md:78 and spec.md:113 require the rename and old-anchor cleanup.

findingDetails.title: Goal plugin is only documented as a Utility command instead of a full FEATURES subsection

findingsNew.title: Active graph metadata still indexes the retired Spec Kit Documentation label
findingsNew.claim: The README rename would still leave this active phase graph metadata advertising the retired Spec Kit Documentation entity, so post-fix memory and graph consumers can continue surfacing the old label after README is corrected.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whole-repo exact rg output. Archived z_archive specs and this review lineage prompts/evidence intentionally quote the old label and are not live cross-references; the graph-metadata hit is active packet metadata.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The metadata entity may be automatically derived from a task that intentionally describes a rename from old to new, but it remains a machine-consumed stale entity after the public rename lands.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if completion tooling regenerates graph metadata after the task wording changes, or if the packet explicitly allows derived metadata to preserve source rename phrases after public docs are corrected.

findingDetails.title: Active graph metadata still indexes the retired Spec Kit Documentation label
findingDetails.scopeProof: Whole-repo exact rg for '#spec-kit-documentation' and 'Spec Kit Documentation' found README.md:33/208 plus active graph-metadata.json:164; archived historical specs and review prompts/evidence were excluded as non-live cross-references.

findingsNew.title: Root Deep Loop docs omit the fan-out permission and guardrail safety posture
findingsNew.claim: The reviewed public Deep Loop section describes hands-off autonomous loops and trustworthy stops, but it does not disclose that fan-out can run OpenCode with --dangerously-skip-permissions, nor does it surface the phase-009 guardrails that bound and observe those autonomous subprocesses.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched README.md and AGENTS*.md for dangerously-skip-permissions, stallWatchdog, stall watchdog, budget cap, cost cap, max_cost_units, lag_ceiling, near-duplicate, default-off, kill-switch, and cooldown; only the unrelated Goal guardrail line appeared in README.md:1233 and AGENTS.md:350 has generic feature-flag language.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The root README may intentionally stay high-level while .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md documents fan-out internals, but the reviewed root README is the same surface advertising autonomous Deep Loop execution and should name or directly link the permission/sandbox boundary.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if root README feature sections are formally marketing-only and the Deep Loop Runtime README is designated as the sole operator contract, or if a reviewed root-surface link explicitly names the permission-bypass and fan-out guardrail posture.

findingDetails.title: Root Deep Loop docs omit the fan-out permission and guardrail safety posture
findingDetails.scopeProof: README.md:780 and README.md:817-818 advertise autonomous Deep Loop execution; reviewed README/AGENTS surfaces do not mention dangerously-skip-permissions, stall watchdog, cost cap, or lag-ceiling guardrails; fanout-run.cjs:1246-1251 and fanout-run.cjs:1280-1291 show native/cli-opencode permission-bypass behavior, with prompt-only lineageDir boundary warning.
affectedSurfaceHints: root README Deep Loop FEATURES section

findingsNew.title: Deep-review iteration 5 has a body/final-line verdict mismatch
findingsNew.claim: iteration-5.md is internally contradictory because its Verdict section states CONDITIONAL while the mandatory parseable final line says Review verdict: PASS.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the deep-review final-line contract and iteration-5 JSONL record; the JSONL reports zero new findings but does not close the four active P1 findings, so no registry evidence supports a clean lineage PASS.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: If final lines were intended to reflect only findings newly introduced by that iteration, PASS could be locally consistent. That conflicts with iteration-5's own body text and this run's active-finding synthesis requirement.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if the reducer/synthesis contract is amended to define final lines as per-iteration-new-findings only and explicitly ignore active registry findings.

findingDetails.title: Deep-review iteration 5 has a body/final-line verdict mismatch
findingDetails.scopeProof: Read iteration-5.md:45-52 and registry lines 6-70; mismatch is confined to iteration-5.md while this iteration's body and final line both use CONDITIONAL.

findingsNew.title: Active graph metadata will not safely self-resolve from checkbox-only completion
findingsNew.claim: The prior expectation that graph-metadata.json will self-resolve after metadata regeneration is incomplete unless source wording is also corrected or generated metadata is explicitly patched after regeneration.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whole-repo #spec-kit-documentation references and found no additional live anchor consumer outside README/spec/review evidence; checked current tasks source and found the retired label still present in active task text.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The generator might ignore completed task rows or quoted old labels, but no reviewed evidence proves that behavior and current metadata already demonstrates the retired label was derived into the entity catalog.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if metadata regeneration is proven to ignore completed task rows or quoted old labels, or if remediation explicitly updates tasks.md:72 wording before regenerating metadata.

findingDetails.title: Active graph metadata will not safely self-resolve from checkbox-only completion
findingDetails.scopeProof: graph-metadata.json:164 carries the retired entity and tasks.md:72 still contains the retired label as source text, so checkbox-only completion plus metadata regeneration is not proven to clear the entity.
